summary: The question of whether classes with polynomial expansion have bounded twin-width remains open as of 2026, confirmed by Bonnet's own open-problems page which lists it with the note 'we are quite far from proving it.' Twin-width IV (arXiv:2102.03117) gives a partial resolution for hereditary classes of ordered graphs, showing bounded twin-width and sub-exponential growth are equivalent in that setting. The related but strictly more general 'small conjecture' (every small class has bounded twin-width) was disproved by Twin-width VII (arXiv:2204.12330), which constructs via a Cayley-graph argument a small class with unbounded twin-width; however, that counterexample does not have polynomial expansion, so the polynomial-expansion question remains open and isolated.

since_posted.title: Twin-width IV: ordered graphs and matrices
since_posted.authors: Édouard Bonnet, Ugo Giocanti, Patrice Ossona de Mendez, Pierre Simon, Stéphan Thomassé, Rémi Watrigant
since_posted.claim: Proves that for hereditary classes of ordered graphs, bounded twin-width is equivalent to sub-exponential (2^{O(n)}) growth, giving a partial resolution of the conjecture in the ordered setting.

since_posted.title: Twin-width VII: groups
since_posted.authors: Édouard Bonnet, Colin Geniet, Romain Tessera, Stéphan Thomassé
since_posted.claim: Disproves the more general 'small conjecture' by constructing (via a finitely generated group with infinite twin-width, Theorem 1.1) a small hereditary class with unbounded twin-width (Corollary 1.4); the counterexample is not a class with polynomial expansion, so the polynomial-expansion question itself remains open.

internal_refs_verified.note: Abstract confirms the paper characterises bounded twin-width for ordered binary structures and relates it to sub-exponential labelled growth; the claimed contribution (partial resolution for ordered graphs) is consistent with the abstract, though the full theorem numbering could not be confirmed from the abstract alone.

Abstract confirms the paper proves groups with polynomial growth (among other classes) have finite twin-width, and constructs an example with infinite twin-width. However, the internal-ref claim that this 'disproves the conjecture' (polynomial expansion → bounded twin-width) is inaccurate: it disproves the more general small conjecture, and Bonnet's current open-problems page explicitly still lists the polynomial-expansion question as open.

search_queries: polynomial expansion bounded twin-width classes graph theory arXiv 2006.09877
twin-width "polynomial expansion" small class conjecture disproved 2022 2023 2024
notes: Bonnet's open-problems page (fetched 2026-05-15) explicitly lists this question as open with the remark 'we are quite far from proving it.' The small conjecture (small ⇒ bounded twin-width) was disproved by Twin-width VII; the polynomial expansion question is a strictly harder sub-question that survives the disproof because the Cayley-graph counterexample lacks polynomial expansion.
